 832→    private fun ASTNode.skipLeadingWhitespaceCommentsAndAnnotations(): ASTNode {
 833→        require(elementType == SECONDARY_CONSTRUCTOR || elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER)
 834→        return findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST)
 835→            ?.let { modifierList ->
 836→                modifierList
 837→                    .children()
 838→                    .firstOrNull { !it.isWhiteSpace() && !it.isPartOfComment() && it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
 839→                    ?: modifierList.nextCodeSibling()
 840→            }
 841→            ?: children().firstOrNull { !it.isWhiteSpace() && !it.isPartOfComment() }
 842→            ?: this
 843→    }
 844→
 845→    private fun ASTNode.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(): ASTNode {
 846→        var fromAstNode: ASTNode? = this
 847→        while (fromAstNode?.prevLeaf() != null &&
 848→            (fromAstNode.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpace() || fromAstNode.prevLeaf()?.isPartOfComment() == true)
 849→        ) {
 850→            fromAstNode = fromAstNode.prevLeaf()
 851→        }
 852→        return fromAstNode!!
 853→    }
 854→
 855→    private fun isPartOfBinaryExpressionWrappedInCondition(node: ASTNode) =
 856→        node
 857→            .parents()
 858→            .takeWhile { it.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION || it.elementType == CONDITION }
 859→            .lastOrNull()
 860→            ?.elementType == CONDITION
 861→
 862→    private fun currentIndent() =
 863→        indentContextStack
 864→            .peekLast()
 865→            .indent()
 866→
 867→    private fun startIndentContext(
 868→        fromAstNode: ASTNode,
 869→        toAstNode: ASTNode = fromAstNode.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 870→        nodeIndent: String = currentIndent(),
 871→        childIndent: String = indentConfig.indent,
 872→        firstChildIndent: String = childIndent,
 873→        lastChildIndent: String = childIndent,
 874→    ): IndentContext =
 875→        IndentContext(
 876→            fromASTNode = fromAstNode,
 877→            toASTNode = toAstNode,
 878→            nodeIndent = nodeIndent,
 879→            firstChildIndent = firstChildIndent,
 880→            childIndent = childIndent,
 881→            lastChildIndent = lastChildIndent,
 882→        ).also { newIndentContext ->
 883→            LOGGER.trace {
 884→                val nodeIndentLevel = indentConfig.indentLevelFrom(newIndentContext.nodeIndent)
 885→                val childIndentLevel = indentConfig.indentLevelFrom(newIndentContext.childIndent)
 886→                "Create new indent context (same as parent) with level ($nodeIndentLevel, $childIndentLevel) for " +
 887→                    "${fromAstNode.elementType}: ${newIndentContext.nodes}"
 888→            }
 889→            indentContextStack.addLast(newIndentContext)
 890→        }
 891→
 892→    override fun afterVisitChildNodes(
 893→        node: ASTNode,
 894→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 895→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 896→    ) {
 897→        while (indentContextStack.peekLast()?.toASTNode == node) {
 898→            LOGGER.trace {
 899→                val indentContext = indentContextStack.peekLast()
 900→                val nodeIndentLevel = indentConfig.indentLevelFrom(indentContext.nodeIndent)
 901→                val childIndentLevel = indentConfig.indentLevelFrom(indentContext.childIndent)
 902→                "Remove indent context with level ($nodeIndentLevel, $childIndentLevel) for ${indentContext.fromASTNode.elementType}: " +
 903→                    "${indentContext.nodes}"
 904→            }
 905→            indentContextStack
 906→                .removeLast()
 907→                .also {
 908→                    LOGGER.trace {
 909→                        val indentContext = indentContextStack.peekLast()
 910→                        val nodeIndentLevel = indentConfig.indentLevelFrom(indentContext.nodeIndent)
 911→                        val childIndentLevel = indentConfig.indentLevelFrom(indentContext.childIndent)
 912→                        "Last indent context with level ($nodeIndentLevel, $childIndentLevel) for " +
 913→                            "${indentContext.fromASTNode.elementType}: ${indentContext.nodes}"
 914→                    }
 915→                }
 916→        }
 917→    }
 918→
 919→    override fun afterLastNode() {
 920→        require(indentContextStack.isEmpty()) {
 921→            indentContextStack
 922→                .joinToString(prefix = "Stack should be empty:\n\t", separator = "\n\t") { it.toString() }
 923→        }
 924→    }
 925→
 926→    private fun visitNewLineIndentation(
 927→        node: ASTNode,
 928→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 929→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 930→    ) {
 931→        if (node.ignoreIndent()) {
 932→            return
 933→        }
 934→
 935→        val normalizedNodeIndent = node.normalizedIndent(emit)
 936→        val expectedIndentation = node.expectedIndent()
 937→        val text = node.text
 938→        val nodeIndent = text.substringAfterLast("\n")
 939→        if (nodeIndent != normalizedNodeIndent || normalizedNodeIndent != expectedIndentation) {
 940→            if (normalizedNodeIndent != expectedIndentation) {
 941→                emit(
 942→                    node.startOffset + text.length - nodeIndent.length,
 943→                    "Unexpected indentation (${normalizedNodeIndent.length}) (should be ${expectedIndentation.length})",
 944→                    true,
 945→                )
 946→            } else {
 947→                // Indentation was at correct level but contained invalid indent characters. This violation has already
 948→                // been emitted.
 949→            }
 950→            LOGGER.trace {
 951→                "Line $line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "changed indentation to ${expectedIndentation.length} " +
 952→                    "(from ${normalizedNodeIndent.length}) for ${node.elementType}: ${node.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()}"
 953→            }
 954→            if (autoCorrect) {
 955→                (node as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(
 956→                    text.substringBeforeLast("\n") + "\n" + expectedIndentation,
 957→                )
 958→            }
 959→        } else {
 960→            node.processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded()
 961→        }
 962→    }
 963→
 964→    private fun ASTNode.ignoreIndent(): Boolean {
 965→        val nextLeaf = nextLeaf()
 966→        if (text.endsWith("\n") && nextLeaf.isStartOfRawStringLiteral()) {
 967→            processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded()
 968→            return true // raw strings (""") are allowed at column 0
 969→        }
 970→
 971→        nextLeaf
 972→            ?.parent(strict = false) { it.psi is PsiComment }
 973→            ?.let { comment ->
 974→                if (text.endsWith("\n")) {
 975→                    processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded()
 976→                    return true // comments are allowed at column 0
 977→                }
 978→                if (comment.textContains('\n') && comment.elementType == BLOCK_COMMENT) {
 979→                    // FIXME: while we cannot assume any kind of layout inside a block comment,
 980→                    // `/*` and `*/` can still be indented
 981→                    processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded()
 982→                    return true
 983→                }
 984→            }
 985→        return false
 986→    }
 987→
 988→    private fun ASTNode?.isStartOfRawStringLiteral(): Boolean = this != null && this.elementType == OPEN_QUOTE && this.text == "\"\"\""
 989→
 990→    private fun ASTNode.processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded() =
 991→        LOGGER.trace { "No indentation change required for $elementType: ${textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()}" }
 992→
 993→    private fun ASTNode.expectedIndent(): String {
 994→        val lastIndexContext = indentContextStack.peekLast()
 995→        val nextLeaf = nextLeaf()
 996→        val adjustedChildIndent =
 997→            when {
 998→                this == lastIndexContext.fromASTNode.firstChildLeafOrSelf() ||
 999→                    nextLeaf == lastIndexContext.fromASTNode.firstChildLeafOrSelf() ->
1000→                    lastIndexContext.firstChildIndent
1001→
1002→                this == lastIndexContext.toASTNode ||
1003→                    nextLeaf == lastIndexContext.toASTNode ->
1004→                    lastIndexContext.lastChildIndent
1005→
1006→                else -> lastIndexContext.childIndent
1007→            }
1008→        return lastIndexContext.nodeIndent + adjustedChildIndent
1009→    }
1010→
1011→    private fun ASTNode.normalizedIndent(emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit): String {
1012→        val nodeIndent = text.substringAfterLast("\n")
1013→        return when (indentConfig.indentStyle) {
1014→            SPACE -> {
1015→                if ('\t' in nodeIndent) {
1016→                    emit(
1017→                        startOffset + text.length - nodeIndent.length,
1018→                        "Unexpected tab character(s)",
1019→                        true,
1020→                    )
1021→                    indentConfig.toNormalizedIndent(nodeIndent)
1022→                } else {
1023→                    nodeIndent
1024→                }
1025→            }
1026→
1027→            TAB -> {
1028→                val acceptableTrailingSpaces = acceptableTrailingSpaces()
1029→                val nodeIndentWithoutAcceptableTrailingSpaces = nodeIndent.removeSuffix(acceptableTrailingSpaces)
1030→                if (' ' in nodeIndentWithoutAcceptableTrailingSpaces) {
1031→                    emit(
1032→                        startOffset + text.length - nodeIndent.length,
1033→                        "Unexpected space character(s)",
1034→                        true,
1035→                    )
1036→                    indentConfig
1037→                        .toNormalizedIndent(nodeIndentWithoutAcceptableTrailingSpaces)
1038→                        .plus(acceptableTrailingSpaces)
1039→                } else {
1040→                    nodeIndent
1041→                }
1042→            }
1043→        }
1044→    }
1045→
1046→    private fun visitWhiteSpaceBeforeClosingQuote(
1047→        node: ASTNode,
1048→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
1049→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
1050→    ) {
1051→        if (!this::stringTemplateIndenter.isInitialized) {
1052→            stringTemplateIndenter = StringTemplateIndenter(indentConfig)
1053→        }
1054→        stringTemplateIndenter.visitClosingQuotes(currentIndent(), node.treeParent, autoCorrect, emit)
1055→    }
1056→
1057→    private fun ASTNode.acceptableTrailingSpaces(): String {
1058→        require(elementType == WHITE_SPACE)
1059→        val acceptableTrailingSpaces =
1060→            when (nextLeaf()?.elementType) {
1061→                KDOC_LEADING_ASTERISK, KDOC_END -> {
1062→                    // The indentation of a KDoc comment contains a space as the last character regardless of the indentation
1063→                    // style (tabs or spaces) except for the starting line of the KDoc comment
1064→                    KDOC_CONTINUATION_INDENT
1065→                }
1066→
1067→                TYPE_CONSTRAINT -> {
1068→                    // 6 spaces (length of "where" keyword plus a separator space) to indent type constraints as below:
1069→                    //    where A1 : RecyclerView.Adapter<V1>,
1070→                    //               A1 : ComposableAdapter.ViewTypeProvider,
1071→                    TYPE_CONSTRAINT_CONTINUATION_INDENT
1072→                }
1073→
1074→                else -> ""
1075→            }
1076→        val nodeIndent = text.substringAfterLast("\n")
1077→        return if (nodeIndent.endsWith(acceptableTrailingSpaces)) {
1078→            return acceptableTrailingSpaces
1079→        } else {
1080→            ""
1081→        }
1082→    }
1083→
1084→    private fun startNoIndentZone(node: ASTNode) =
1085→        IndentContext(
1086→            fromASTNode = node,
1087→            nodeIndent = "",
1088→            firstChildIndent = "",
1089→            childIndent = "",
1090→            lastChildIndent = "",
1091→            activated = true,
1092→        )
1093→
1094→    private companion object {
1095→        const val KDOC_CONTINUATION_INDENT = " "
1096→        const val TYPE_CONSTRAINT_CONTINUATION_INDENT = "      " // Length of keyword "where" plus separating space
1097→        val CHAINABLE_EXPRESSION = setOf(DOT_QUALIFIED_EXPRESSION, SAFE_ACCESS_EXPRESSION)
1098→    }
1099→
1100→    private data class IndentContext(
1101→        /**
1102→         * The node on which the indent context starts.
1103→         */
1104→        val fromASTNode: ASTNode,
1105→        /**
1106→         * The node at which the indent context ends. If null, then the context ends at the last child leaf of the node
1107→         * on which the indent context starts.
1108→         */
1109→        val toASTNode: ASTNode = fromASTNode.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
1110→        /**
1111→         * Cumulative indentation for the node. Normally this should be equal to a multiple of the
1112→         * 'indentConfig.indentStyle' to ensure a consistent indentation style.
1113→         */
1114→        val nodeIndent: String,
1115→        /**
1116→         * Additional indentation for first child node. Normally this should be equal to the 'indentConfig.indent' to
1117→         * ensure a consistent indentation style. In very limited cases when the default indentation is set to 'tab' it
1118→         * is still needed to adjust the last indentation using spaces.
1119→         */
1120→        val firstChildIndent: String,
1121→        /**
1122→         * Additional indentation for child nodes. Normally this should be equal to the 'indentConfig.indent' to ensure
1123→         * a consistent indentation style. In very limited cases when the default indentation is set to 'tab' it is
1124→         * still needed to adjust the last indentation using spaces.
1125→         */
1126→        val childIndent: String,
1127→        /**
1128→         * Additional indentation for last child node. Normally this should be equal to the 'indentConfig.indent' to
1129→         * ensure a consistent indentation style. In very limited cases when the default indentation is set to 'tab' it
1130→         * is still needed to adjust the last indentation using spaces.
1131→         */
1132→        val lastChildIndent: String,
1133→        /**
1134→         * True when the indentation level of this context is activated
1135→         */
1136→        val activated: Boolean = false,
1137→    ) {
1138→        val nodes: String
1139→            get() =
1140→                fromASTNode
1141→                    .prevLeaf() // The 'fromAstNode' itself needs to be returned by '.leaves()' call as well
1142→                    ?.leaves()
1143→                    .orEmpty()
1144→                    .takeWhile {
1145→                        // The 'toAstNode' itself needs to be included as well
1146→                        it != toASTNode.nextLeaf()
1147→                    }
1148→                    .joinToString(separator = "") { it.text }
1149→                    .textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()
1150→
1151→        fun indent() =
1152→            if (activated) {
1153→                nodeIndent + childIndent
1154→            } else {
1155→                nodeIndent
1156→            }
1157→
1158→        fun prevCodeLeaf() = fromASTNode.prevCodeLeaf()!!
1159→    }
1160→}
1161→
1162→private fun ASTNode.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline() = text.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()
1163→
1164→private fun String.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline(): String {
1165→    val (prefix, suffix) =
1166→        if (this.all { it.isWhitespace() }) {
1167→            Pair("[", "]")
1168→        } else {
1169→            Pair("", "")
1170→        }
1171→    return prefix
1172→        .plus(
1173→            this
1174→                .replace("\t", "\\t")
1175→                .replace("\n", "\\n"),
1176→        ).plus(suffix)
1177→}
1178→
1179→private class StringTemplateIndenter(private val indentConfig: IndentConfig) {
1180→    fun visitClosingQuotes(
1181→        expectedIndent: String,
1182→        node: ASTNode,
1183→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
1184→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
1185→    ) {
1186→        require(node.elementType == STRING_TEMPLATE)
1187→        node
1188→            .let { it.psi as KtStringTemplateExpression }
1189→            .takeIf { it.isFollowedByTrimIndent() || it.isFollowedByTrimMargin() }
1190→            ?.takeIf { it.isMultiLine() }
1191→            ?.let { _ ->
1192→                if (node.containsMixedIndentationCharacters()) {
1193→                    // It can not be determined with certainty how mixed indentation characters should be interpreted.
1194→                    // The trimIndent function handles tabs and spaces equally (one tabs equals one space) while the user
1195→                    // might expect that the tab size in the indentation is more than one space.
1196→                    emit(
1197→                        node.startOffset,
1198→                        "Indentation of multiline string should not contain both tab(s) and space(s)",
1199→                        false,
1200→                    )
1201→                    return
1202→                }
1203→
1204→                val prefixLength = node.getCommonPrefixLength()
1205→                val correctedExpectedIndent =
1206→                    if (node.prevLeaf()?.text == "\n") {
1207→                        // In case the opening quotes are placed at the start of the line, then the closing quotes
1208→                        // should have no indent as well.
1209→                        ""
1210→                    } else {
1211→                        expectedIndent
1212→                    }
1213→                node.children()
1214→                    .forEach {
1215→                        if (it.prevLeaf()?.text == "\n" &&
1216→                            (
1217→                                it.isLiteralStringTemplateEntry() ||

1250→                                    true,

1269→    /**
1270→     * Get the length of the indent which is shared by all lines inside the string template except for the indent of
1271→     * the closing quotes.

1273→    private fun ASTNode.getCommonPrefixLength() =
1274→        children()
1275→            .filterNot { it.elementType == OPEN_QUOTE }
1276→            .filterNot { it.elementType == CLOSING_QUOTE }
1277→            .filter { it.prevLeaf()?.text == "\n" }
1278→            .filterNot { it.text == "\n" }
1279→            .let { indents ->
1280→                val indentsExceptBlankIndentBeforeClosingQuote =
1281→                    indents
1282→                        .filterNot { it.isIndentBeforeClosingQuote() }
1283→                if (indentsExceptBlankIndentBeforeClosingQuote.count() > 0) {
1284→                    indentsExceptBlankIndentBeforeClosingQuote
1285→                } else {
1286→                    indents

1289→            .map { it.text.indentLength() }

1301→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isMultiLine(): Boolean {
1302→        for (child in node.children()) {
1303→            if (child.elementType == LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY) {
1304→                val v = child.text
1305→                if (v == "\n") {
1306→                    return true
1307→                }
1308→            }
1309→        }

1313→    private fun ASTNode.containsMixedIndentationCharacters(): Boolean {
1314→        assert((this.psi as KtStringTemplateExpression).isMultiLine())
1315→        val nonBlankLines =
1316→            this
1317→                .text
1318→                .split("\n")
1319→                .filterNot { it.startsWith("\"\"\"") }
1320→                .filterNot { it.endsWith("\"\"\"") }
1321→                .filterNot { it.isBlank() }
1322→        val prefixLength = nonBlankLines.minOfOrNull { it.indentLength() } ?: 0
1323→        val distinctIndentCharacters =
1324→            nonBlankLines
1325→                .joinToString(separator = "") {
1326→                    it.splitIndentAt(prefixLength).first
1327→                }
1328→                .toCharArray()
1329→                .distinct()
1330→                .count()
1331→        return distinctIndentCharacters > 1
1332→    }
1333→
1334→    private fun ASTNode.isIndentBeforeClosingQuote() =
1335→        elementType == CLOSING_QUOTE || (text.isBlank() && nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == CLOSING_QUOTE)
1336→
1337→    private fun ASTNode.isLiteralStringTemplateEntry() = elementType == LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY && text != "\n"
1338→
1339→    private fun ASTNode.isVariableStringTemplateEntry() =
1340→        elementType == LONG_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY || elementType == SHORT_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY
1341→
1342→    private fun ASTNode.isClosingQuote() = elementType == CLOSING_QUOTE

1344→    private fun String.indentLength() = indexOfFirst { !it.isWhitespace() }.let { if (it == -1) length else it }
1345→
1346→    /**
1347→     * Splits the string at the given index or at the first non-white space character before that index. The returned pair
1348→     * consists of the indentation and the second part contains the remainder. Note that the second part still can start
1349→     * with whitespace characters in case the original strings starts with more white space characters than the requested
1350→     * split index.
1351→     */
1352→    private fun String.splitIndentAt(index: Int): Pair<String, String> {
1353→        assert(index >= 0)
1354→        val firstNonWhitespaceIndex =
1355→            indexOfFirst { !it.isWhitespace() }.let {
1356→                if (it == -1) {
1357→                    this.length
1358→                } else {
1359→                    it
1360→                }
1361→            }
1362→        val safeIndex = kotlin.math.min(firstNonWhitespaceIndex, index)
1363→        return Pair(
1364→            first = this.take(safeIndex),
1365→            second = this.substring(safeIndex),
1366→        )
1367→    }
1368→
1369→    private fun ASTNode.getFirstElementOnSameLine(): ASTNode {
1370→        val firstLeafOnLine = prevLeaf { it.text == "\n" }
1371→        return if (firstLeafOnLine == null) {
1372→            this
1373→        } else {
1374→            firstLeafOnLine.nextLeaf(includeEmpty = true) ?: this
1375→        }
1376→    }
1377→
1378→    private fun ASTNode.isFirstNonBlankElementOnLine(): Boolean {
1379→        var node: ASTNode? = getFirstElementOnSameLine()
1380→        while (node != null && node != this && node.text.isWhitespace()) {
1381→            node = node.nextLeaf()
1382→        }
1383→        return node != this
1384→    }
1385→
1386→    private fun String.isWhitespace() = none { !it.isWhitespace() }
1387→}

 31→/**
 32→ * Lints and formats the spacing before and after the angle brackets of a type parameter list.
 33→ */
 34→public class TypeParameterListSpacingRule :
 35→    StandardRule(
 36→        id = "type-parameter-list-spacing",
 37→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 38→            setOf(
 39→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 40→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 41→            ),
 42→    ),
 43→    Rule.Experimental {
 44→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 45→
 46→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 47→        indentConfig = IndentConfig(
 48→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 49→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 50→        )
 51→    }
 52→
 53→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 54→        node: ASTNode,
 55→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 56→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 57→    ) {
 58→        if (node.elementType != TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST) {
 59→            return
 60→        }
 61→
 62→        when (node.treeParent.elementType) {
 63→            CLASS -> visitClassDeclaration(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 64→            TYPEALIAS -> visitTypeAliasDeclaration(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 65→            FUN -> visitFunctionDeclaration(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 66→        }
 67→        visitInsideTypeParameterList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 68→    }
 69→
 70→    private fun visitClassDeclaration(
 71→        node: ASTNode,
 72→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 73→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 74→    ) {
 75→        // No white space expected between class name and parameter list
 76→        //     class Bar <T>
 77→        node
 78→            .prevSibling()
 79→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
 80→            ?.let { visitWhitespace(it, autoCorrect, emit) }
 81→
 82→        // No white space expected between parameter type list and the constructor except when followed by compound
 83→        // constructor
 84→        //     class Bar<T> (...)
 85→        node
 86→            .nextSibling()
 87→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE && it.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR }
 88→            ?.let { whiteSpace ->
 89→                if (whiteSpace.nextCodeSibling()?.findChildByType(CONSTRUCTOR_KEYWORD) != null) {
 90→                    // Single space expect before (modifier list of) constructor
 91→                    //    class Bar<T> constructor(...)
 92→                    //    class Bar<T> actual constructor(...)
 93→                    //    class Bar<T> @SomeAnnotation constructor(...)
 94→                    singleSpaceExpected(whiteSpace, autoCorrect, emit)
 95→                } else {
 96→                    visitWhitespace(whiteSpace, autoCorrect, emit)
 97→                }
 98→            }
 99→
100→        // No white space expected between parameter type list and class body when constructor is missing
101→        //    class Bar<T> {
102→        node
103→            .nextSibling()
104→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE && it.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == CLASS_BODY }
105→            ?.let { singleSpaceExpected(it, autoCorrect, emit) }
106→    }

108→    private fun visitTypeAliasDeclaration(
109→        node: ASTNode,
110→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
111→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
112→    ) {
113→        // No white space expected between typealias keyword name and parameter list
114→        //     typealias Bar <T>
115→        node
116→            .prevSibling()
117→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
118→            ?.let { visitWhitespace(it, autoCorrect, emit) }
119→
120→        // No white space expected between parameter type list and equals sign
121→        //    typealias Bar<T> = ...
122→        node
123→            .nextSibling()
124→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE && it.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == EQ }
125→            ?.let { singleSpaceExpected(it, autoCorrect, emit) }
126→    }
127→
128→    private fun visitFunctionDeclaration(
129→        node: ASTNode,
130→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
131→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
132→    ) {
133→        // Single space expected before type parameter list of function
134→        //    fun<T> foo(...)
135→        node
136→            .prevLeaf(includeEmpty = true)
137→            ?.let { prevLeaf ->
138→                if (prevLeaf.elementType == WHITE_SPACE) {
139→                    singleSpaceExpected(prevLeaf, autoCorrect, emit)
140→                } else {
141→                    singleSpaceExpected(node.firstChildNode, autoCorrect, emit)
142→                }
143→            }
144→
145→        // Single space expected after type parameter list of function
146→        //   fun <T>foo(...)
147→        //   fun <T>List<T>foo(...)
148→        node
149→            .lastChildNode
150→            .nextLeaf(includeEmpty = true)
151→            ?.let { nextSibling ->
152→                singleSpaceExpected(nextSibling, autoCorrect, emit)
153→            }
154→    }
155→
156→    private fun visitInsideTypeParameterList(
157→        node: ASTNode,
158→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
159→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
160→    ) {
161→        node
162→            .findChildByType(LT)
163→            ?.nextSibling()
164→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
165→            ?.let {
166→                val expectedWhitespace =
167→                    if (node.textContains('\n')) {
168→                        node.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent)
169→                    } else {
170→                        ""
171→                    }
172→                visitWhitespace(it, autoCorrect, emit, expectedWhitespace)
173→            }
174→
175→        node
176→            .findChildByType(GT)
177→            ?.prevSibling()
178→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
179→            ?.let {
180→                val expectedWhitespace =
181→                    if (node.textContains('\n')) {
182→                        node.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent)
183→                    } else {
184→                        ""
185→                    }
186→                visitWhitespace(it, autoCorrect, emit, expectedWhitespace)
187→            }
188→    }
189→
190→    private fun visitWhitespace(
191→        node: ASTNode,
192→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
193→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
194→        expectedWhitespace: String = "",
195→    ) {
196→        if (node.text == expectedWhitespace) {
197→            return
198→        }
199→
200→        when {
201→            expectedWhitespace.isEmpty() -> {
202→                emit(
203→                    node.startOffset,
204→                    "No whitespace expected",
205→                    true,
206→                )
207→                if (autoCorrect) {
208→                    node.treeParent.removeChild(node)
209→                }
210→            }
211→
212→            expectedWhitespace.startsWith("\n") -> {
213→                emit(
214→                    node.startOffset,
215→                    "Expected a newline",
216→                    true,
217→                )
218→                if (autoCorrect) {
219→                    (node as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(expectedWhitespace)
220→                }
221→            }
222→
223→            expectedWhitespace == " " -> {
224→                emit(
225→                    node.startOffset,
226→                    "Expected a single space",
227→                    true,
228→                )
229→                if (autoCorrect) {
230→                    (node as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(expectedWhitespace)
231→                }
232→            }
233→        }
234→    }
235→
236→    private fun singleSpaceExpected(
237→        node: ASTNode,
238→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
239→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
240→    ) {
241→        when {
242→            node.text == " " -> Unit
243→            node.textContains('\n') -> {
244→                emit(
245→                    node.startOffset,
246→                    "Expected a single space instead of newline",
247→                    true,
248→                )
249→                if (autoCorrect) {
250→                    (node as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(" ")
251→                }
252→            }
253→            else -> {
254→                emit(
255→                    node.startOffset,
256→                    "Expected a single space",
257→                    true,
258→                )
259→                if (autoCorrect) {
260→                    node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
261→                }
262→            }
263→        }
264→    }
265→}

267→public val TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST_SPACING_RULE_ID: RuleId = TypeParameterListSpacingRule().ruleId

 86→/**
 87→ * This rule inserts missing newlines (e.g. between parentheses of a multi-line function call). This logic previously
 88→ * was part of the IndentationRule (phase 1).
 89→ *
 90→ * Whenever a linebreak is inserted, this rules assumes that the parent node it indented correctly. So the indentation
 91→ * is fixed with respect to indentation of the parent. This is just a simple best effort for the case that the
 92→ * indentation rule is not run.
 93→ */
 94→public class WrappingRule :
 95→    StandardRule(
 96→        id = "wrapping",
 97→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 98→            setOf(
 99→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
100→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
101→                MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
102→            ),
103→        visitorModifiers =
104→            setOf(
105→                RunAfterRule(
106→                    ruleId = ANNOTATION_RULE_ID,
107→                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
108→                ),
109→            ),
110→    ) {
111→    private var line = 1
112→    private var indentConfig = DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
113→    private var maxLineLength: Int = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
114→
115→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
116→        line = 1
117→        indentConfig = IndentConfig(
118→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
119→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
120→        )
121→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
122→    }
123→
124→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
125→        node: ASTNode,
126→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
127→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
128→    ) {
129→        when (node.elementType) {
130→            BLOCK -> beforeVisitBlock(node, autoCorrect, emit)
131→            LPAR, LBRACKET -> rearrangeBlock(node, autoCorrect, emit) // TODO: LT
132→            SUPER_TYPE_LIST -> rearrangeSuperTypeList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
133→            VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST, VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST -> rearrangeValueList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
134→            TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST, TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST -> rearrangeTypeArgumentList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
135→//            TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST -> rearrangeTypeParameterList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
136→            ARROW -> rearrangeArrow(node, autoCorrect, emit)
137→            WHITE_SPACE -> line += node.text.count { it == '\n' }
138→            CLOSING_QUOTE -> rearrangeClosingQuote(node, autoCorrect, emit)
139→        }
140→    }

142→    private fun beforeVisitBlock(
143→        node: ASTNode,
144→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
145→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
146→    ) {
147→        require(node.elementType == BLOCK)
148→
149→        val startOfBlock = node.prevLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
150→        if (startOfBlock?.elementType != LBRACE) {
151→            return
152→        }
153→        val blockIsPrecededByWhitespaceContainingNewline = startOfBlock.nextLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
154→        val endOfBlock = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf().nextLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
155→        val blockIsFollowedByWhitespaceContainingNewline = endOfBlock?.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
156→        val wrapBlock =
157→            when {
158→                startOfBlock.isPartOf(LONG_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY) -> {
159→                    // String template inside raw string literal may exceed the maximum line length
160→                    false
161→                }
162→                blockIsPrecededByWhitespaceContainingNewline -> false
163→                node.textContains('\n') || blockIsFollowedByWhitespaceContainingNewline -> {
164→                    // A multiline block should always be wrapped unless it starts with an EOL comment
165→                    node.firstChildLeafOrSelf().elementType != EOL_COMMENT
166→                }
167→                maxLineLength != MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF -> {
168→                    val lengthUntilBeginOfLine =
169→                        node
170→                            .leaves(false)
171→                            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
172→                            .sumOf { it.textLength }
173→                    val lengthUntilEndOfLine =
174→                        node
175→                            .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
176→                            .leavesIncludingSelf()
177→                            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
178→                            .sumOf { it.textLength }
179→                    lengthUntilBeginOfLine + lengthUntilEndOfLine > maxLineLength
180→                }
181→                else -> false
182→            }
183→        if (wrapBlock) {
184→            startOfBlock
185→                .takeIf { !it.nextLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
186→                ?.let { leafNodeBeforeBlock ->
187→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
188→                        leafNodeBeforeBlock,
189→                        autoCorrect,
190→                        emit,
191→                    )
192→                }
193→        }
194→    }
195→
196→    private fun rearrangeBlock(
197→        node: ASTNode,
198→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
199→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
200→    ) {
201→        val rElementType = MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP[node.elementType]
202→        var newlineInBetween = false
203→        var parameterListInBetween = false
204→        var numberOfArgs = 0
205→        var firstArg: ASTNode? = null
206→        // matching ), ] or }
207→        val r = node.nextSibling {
208→            val isValueArgument = it.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT
209→            val hasLineBreak = if (isValueArgument) it.hasLineBreak(LAMBDA_EXPRESSION, FUN) else it.hasLineBreak()
210→            newlineInBetween = newlineInBetween || hasLineBreak
211→            parameterListInBetween = parameterListInBetween || it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST
212→            if (isValueArgument) {
213→                numberOfArgs++
214→                firstArg = it
215→            }
216→            it.elementType == rElementType
217→        }!!
218→        if (
219→            !newlineInBetween ||
220→            // keep { p ->
221→            // }
222→            (node.elementType == LBRACE && parameterListInBetween) ||
223→            // keep ({
224→            // }) and (object : C {
225→            // })
226→            (
227→                numberOfArgs == 1 &&
228→                    firstArg?.firstChildNode?.elementType
229→                        ?.let { it == OBJECT_LITERAL || it == LAMBDA_EXPRESSION } == true
230→                )
231→        ) {
232→            return
233→        }
234→        if (node.isPartOfForLoopConditionWithMultilineExpression()) {
235→            // keep:
236→            // for (foo in listOf(
237→            //     "foo-1",
238→            //     "foo-2"
239→            // )) { ... }
240→            // but reject:
241→            // for (
242→            //     foo in listOf(
243→            //         "foo-1",
244→            //         "foo-2"
245→            //     )
246→            // ) { ... }
247→            return
248→        }
249→        if (!node.nextCodeLeaf()?.prevLeaf {
250→                // Skip comments, whitespace, and empty nodes
251→                !it.isPartOfComment() &&
252→                    !it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() &&
253→                    it.textLength > 0
254→            }.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
255→            // IDEA quirk:
256→            // if (true &&
257→            //     true
258→            // ) {
259→            // }
260→            // instead of
261→            // if (
262→            //     true &&
263→            //     true
264→            // ) {
265→            // }
266→            node.treeNext?.elementType != CONDITION
267→        ) {
268→            requireNewlineAfterLeaf(node, autoCorrect, emit)
269→        }
270→        if (!r.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
271→            requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(r, autoCorrect, emit, node.treeParent.indent())
272→        }
273→    }

275→    private fun rearrangeSuperTypeList(
276→        node: ASTNode,
277→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
278→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
279→    ) {
280→        val entries = (node.psi as KtSuperTypeList).entries
281→        if (
282→            node.textContains('\n') &&
283→            entries.size > 1 &&
284→            // e.g.
285→            //
286→            // class A : B, C,
287→            //     D
288→            // or
289→            // class A : B, C({
290→            // }), D
291→            //
292→            // but not
293→            //
294→            // class A : B, C, D({
295→            // })
296→            !(
297→                entries.dropLast(1).all { it.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_ENTRY } &&
298→                    entries.last().elementType == SUPER_TYPE_CALL_ENTRY
299→                )
300→        ) {
301→            // put space after :
302→            if (!node.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
303→                val colon = node.prevCodeLeaf()!!
304→                if (
305→                    !colon.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
306→                    colon.prevCodeLeaf().let { it?.elementType != RPAR || !it.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
307→                ) {
308→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(colon, autoCorrect, emit)
309→                }
310→            }
311→            // put entries on separate lines
312→            // TODO: group emit()s below with the one above into one (similar to ParameterListWrappingRule)
313→            for (c in node.children()) {
314→                if (c.elementType == COMMA &&
315→                    !c.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
316→                    !c.isFollowedByCommentOnSameLine()
317→                ) {
318→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
319→                        nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired = c,
320→                        autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
321→                        emit = emit,
322→                        indent = node.indent(),
323→                    )
324→                }
325→            }
326→        }
327→    }
328→
329→    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByCommentOnSameLine() =
330→        nextLeaf { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() }
331→            ?.isPartOfComment() == true
332→
333→    private fun rearrangeValueList(
334→        node: ASTNode,
335→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
336→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
337→    ) {
338→        for (c in node.children()) {
339→            val hasLineBreak =
340→                when (c.elementType) {
341→                    VALUE_ARGUMENT -> c.hasLineBreak(LAMBDA_EXPRESSION, FUN)
342→                    VALUE_PARAMETER, ANNOTATION -> c.hasLineBreak()
343→                    else -> false
344→                }
345→            if (hasLineBreak) {
346→                // rearrange
347→                //
348→                // a, b, value(
349→                // ), c, d
350→                //
351→                // to
352→                //
353→                // a, b,
354→                // value(
355→                // ),
356→                // c, d
357→
358→                // insert \n in front of multi-line value
359→                val prevSibling = c.prevSibling { it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE }
360→                if (
361→                    prevSibling?.elementType == COMMA &&
362→                    !prevSibling.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
363→                ) {
364→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(prevSibling, autoCorrect, emit)
365→                }
366→                // insert \n after multi-line value
367→                val nextSibling = c.nextSibling { it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE }
368→                val hasDestructuringDeclarationAsLastValueParameter =
369→                    c.isLastValueParameter() && c.firstChildNode.elementType == DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION
370→                if (
371→                    nextSibling?.elementType == COMMA &&
372→                    !hasDestructuringDeclarationAsLastValueParameter &&
373→                    !nextSibling.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
374→                    // value(
375→                    // ), // a comment
376→                    // c, d
377→                    nextSibling.treeNext?.treeNext?.psi !is PsiComment
378→                ) {
379→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(nextSibling, autoCorrect, emit)
380→                }
381→            }
382→        }
383→    }
384→
385→    private fun ASTNode.isLastValueParameter() =
386→        elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER &&
387→            siblings().none { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
388→
389→    private fun rearrangeTypeArgumentList(
390→        node: ASTNode,
391→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
392→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
393→    ) {
394→        if (node.textContains('\n')) {
395→            // Each type projection must be preceded with a whitespace containing a newline
396→            node
397→                .children()
398→                .filter { it.elementType == TYPE_PROJECTION || it.elementType == TYPE_PARAMETER }
399→                .forEach { typeProjection ->
400→                    typeProjection
401→                        .prevSibling { !it.isPartOfComment() }
402→                        .let { prevSibling ->
403→                            if (prevSibling?.elementType == LT || prevSibling.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline()) {
404→                                emit(typeProjection.startOffset, "A newline was expected before '${typeProjection.text}'", true)
405→                                if (autoCorrect) {
406→                                    typeProjection.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(
407→                                        node.treeParent.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent),
408→                                    )
409→                                }
410→                            }
411→                        }
412→                }
413→
414→            // After the last type projection a whitespace containing a newline must exist
415→            node
416→                .findChildByType(GT)
417→                ?.let { closingAngle ->
418→                    val prevSibling = closingAngle.prevSibling { !it.isPartOfComment() }
419→                    if (prevSibling?.elementType != WHITE_SPACE || prevSibling.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline()) {
420→                        emit(closingAngle.startOffset, "A newline was expected before '${closingAngle.text}'", true)
421→                        if (autoCorrect) {
422→                            closingAngle.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(
423→                                node.treeParent.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent),
424→                            )
425→                        }
426→                    }
427→                }
428→
429→            Unit
430→        }
431→    }
432→
433→    private fun rearrangeClosingQuote(
434→        node: ASTNode,
435→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
436→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
437→    ) {
438→        node
439→            .treeParent
440→            .takeIf { it.elementType == STRING_TEMPLATE }
441→            ?.let { it.psi as KtStringTemplateExpression }
442→            ?.takeIf { it.isMultiLine() }
443→            ?.takeIf { it.isFollowedByTrimIndent() || it.isFollowedByTrimMargin() }
444→            ?.takeIf { node.treePrev.text.isNotBlank() }
445→            ?.let {
446→                // rewriting
447→                // """
448→                //     text
449→                // _""".trimIndent()
450→                // to
451→                // """
452→                //     text
453→                // _
454→                // """.trimIndent()
455→                emit(
456→                    node.startOffset,
457→                    "Missing newline before \"\"\"",
458→                    true,
459→                )
460→                if (autoCorrect) {
461→                    node as LeafPsiElement
462→                    node.rawInsertBeforeMe(LeafPsiElement(LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY, "\n"))
463→                }
464→                LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline before (closing) \"\"\"" }
465→            }
466→    }
467→
468→    private fun mustBeFollowedByNewline(node: ASTNode): Boolean {
469→        // find EOL token (last token before \n)
470→        // if token is in lTokenSet
471→        //     find matching rToken
472→        //     return true if there is no newline after the rToken
473→        // return false
474→        val nextCodeSibling = node.nextCodeSibling() // e.g. BINARY_EXPRESSION
475→        var lToken = nextCodeSibling?.nextLeaf { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }?.prevCodeLeaf()
476→        if (lToken != null && lToken.elementType !in LTOKEN_SET) {
477→            // special cases:
478→            // x = y.f({ z ->
479→            // })
480→            // x = y.f(0, 1,
481→            // 2, 3)
482→            lToken = lToken.prevLeaf { it.elementType in LTOKEN_SET || it == node }
483→        }
484→        if (lToken != null && lToken.elementType in LTOKEN_SET) {
485→            val rElementType = MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP[lToken.elementType]
486→            val rToken = lToken.nextSibling { it.elementType == rElementType }
487→            return rToken?.treeParent == lToken.treeParent
488→        }
489→        if (nextCodeSibling?.textContains('\n') == false) {
490→            return true
491→        }
492→        return false
493→    }
494→
495→    private fun rearrangeArrow(
496→        node: ASTNode,
497→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
498→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
499→    ) {
500→        val p = node.treeParent
501→        if (
502→            // check
503→            // `{ p -> ... }`
504→            // and
505→            // `when { m -> ... }`
506→            // only
507→            p.elementType.let { it != FUNCTION_LITERAL && it != WHEN_ENTRY } ||
508→            // ... and only if expression after -> spans multiple lines
509→            !p.textContains('\n') ||
510→            // permit
511→            // when {
512→            //     m -> 0 + d({
513→            //     })
514→            // }
515→            (p.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY && mustBeFollowedByNewline(node)) ||
516→            // permit
517→            // when (this) {
518→            //     in 0x1F600..0x1F64F, // Emoticons
519→            //     0x200D // Zero-width Joiner
520→            //     -> true
521→            // }
522→            (p.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY && node.prevLeaf()?.textContains('\n') == true)
523→        ) {
524→            return
525→        }
526→        if (!node.nextCodeLeaf()?.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
527→            requireNewlineAfterLeaf(node, autoCorrect, emit)
528→        }
529→        val r = node.nextSibling { it.elementType == RBRACE } ?: return
530→        if (!r.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
531→            requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(r, autoCorrect, emit, node.indent())
532→        }
533→    }
534→
535→    private fun requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(
536→        node: ASTNode,
537→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
538→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
539→        indent: String,
540→    ) {
541→        emit(
542→            node.startOffset - 1,
543→            """Missing newline before "${node.text}"""",
544→            true,
545→        )
546→        LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + ((if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline before ${node.text}") }
547→        if (autoCorrect) {
548→            node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(indent)
549→        }
550→    }
551→
552→    private fun requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
553→        nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired: ASTNode,
554→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
555→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
556→        indent: String? = null,
557→        nodeToFix: ASTNode = nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired,
558→    ) {
559→        emit(
560→            nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.startOffset + 1,
561→            """Missing newline after "${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"""",
562→            true,
563→        )
564→        LOGGER.trace {
565→            "$line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline after ${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"
566→        }
567→        if (autoCorrect) {
568→            val tempIndent = indent ?: (nodeToFix.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent))
569→            nodeToFix.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(tempIndent)
570→        }
571→    }
572→
573→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isMultiLine(): Boolean {
574→        for (child in node.children()) {
575→            if (child.elementType == LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY) {
576→                val v = child.text
577→                if (v == "\n") {
578→                    return true
579→                }
580→            }
581→        }
582→        return false
583→    }
584→
585→    private fun ASTNode.hasLineBreak(vararg ignoreElementTypes: IElementType): Boolean {
586→        if (isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) return true
587→        return if (ignoreElementTypes.isEmpty()) {
588→            textContains('\n')
589→        } else {
590→            elementType !in ignoreElementTypes &&
591→                children().any { c -> c.textContains('\n') && c.elementType !in ignoreElementTypes }
592→        }
593→    }
594→
595→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedByTrimIndent() = isFollowedBy("trimIndent()")
596→
597→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedByTrimMargin() = isFollowedBy("trimMargin()")
598→
599→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedBy(callExpressionName: String) =
600→        this.node.nextSibling { it.elementType != DOT }
601→            .let { it?.elementType == CALL_EXPRESSION && it.text == callExpressionName }
602→
603→    /**
604→     *  Allow for-statement in which only the expression contains a newline:
605→     *     for (foo in listOf(
606→     *         "foo-1",
607→     *         "foo-2"
608→     *     )) { ... }
609→     * but reject:
610→     *     for (
611→     *         foo in listOf(
612→     *             "foo-1",
613→     *             "foo-2"
614→     *         )
615→     *     ) { ... }
616→     */
617→    private fun ASTNode.isPartOfForLoopConditionWithMultilineExpression(): Boolean {
618→        if (treeParent.elementType != ElementType.FOR) {
619→            return false
620→        }
621→        if (this.elementType != LPAR) {
622→            return treeParent.findChildByType(LPAR)!!.isPartOfForLoopConditionWithMultilineExpression()
623→        }
624→        require(elementType == LPAR) {
625→            "Node should be the LPAR of the FOR loop"
626→        }
627→
628→        var node: ASTNode? = this
629→        while (node != null && node.elementType != RPAR) {
630→            if (node.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
631→                return false
632→            }
633→            node = node.nextSibling()
634→        }
635→        return true
636→    }
637→
638→    override fun afterVisitChildNodes(
639→        node: ASTNode,
640→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
641→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
642→    ) {
643→        if (node.elementType == BLOCK) {
644→            val startOfBlock = node.prevLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
645→            if (startOfBlock?.elementType != LBRACE) {
646→                return
647→            }
648→            val blockIsPrecededByWhitespaceContainingNewline = startOfBlock.nextLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
649→            val endOfBlock = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf().nextLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
650→            val blockIsFollowedByWhitespaceContainingNewline = endOfBlock?.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
651→            val wrapBlock =
652→                !blockIsFollowedByWhitespaceContainingNewline && (
653→                    blockIsPrecededByWhitespaceContainingNewline || node.textContains('\n')
654→                    )
655→            if (wrapBlock && endOfBlock != null) {
656→                requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(
657→                    endOfBlock,
658→                    autoCorrect,
659→                    emit,
660→                    node.treeParent.indent(),
661→                )
662→            }
663→        }
664→    }
665→
666→    private companion object {
667→        private val LTOKEN_SET = TokenSet.create(LPAR, LBRACE, LBRACKET, LT)
668→        private val RTOKEN_SET = TokenSet.create(RPAR, RBRACE, RBRACKET, GT)
669→        private val MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP =
670→            LTOKEN_SET.types.zip(
671→                RTOKEN_SET.types,
672→            ).toMap()
673→    }
674→}

 71→/**
 72→ * AssertJ style assertion for verifying KtLint rules. This class is intended to be used as follows:
 73→ *
 74→ * ```
 75→ * // Create an assertThat assertion for a specific rule or set of rules. If possible define it at the class level
 76→ * val someRuleAssertThat = SomeRule().assertThat()
 77→ *
 78→ * @Test
 79→ * fun `Some test`() {
 80→ *     val code = .... // Original code to be formatted
 81→ *     val formattedCode = .... // The code as it should be formatted
 82→ *     someRuleAssertThat(code)
 83→ *         .hasLintViolation(1, 1, "some-error-description")
 84→ *         .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
 85→ * }
 86→ * ```
 87→ */
 88→@Suppress("MemberVisibilityCanBePrivate")
 89→public class KtLintAssertThat(
 90→    /**
 91→     * Provider of a rule which is the subject of the test, e.g. the rule for which the AssertThat is created.
 92→     */

100→     * that lint errors for those rules are suppressed.
101→     */
102→    private val additionalRuleProviders: MutableSet<RuleProvider>,
103→    /**
104→     * EditorConfig properties to be applied by default when linting/formatting the code.
105→     */
106→    private val editorConfigProperties: MutableSet<Pair<EditorConfigProperty<*>, *>>,
107→) {
108→    private var filePath: String? = null
109→    private var kotlinScript = false

111→    /**
112→     * Set the [EditorConfigOverride] properties to be used by the rule. This function can be called multiple times.
113→     * Properties which have been set before, are silently overwritten with the new vale.
114→     */
115→    public fun withEditorConfigOverride(vararg properties: Pair<EditorConfigProperty<*>, *>): KtLintAssertThat {
116→        editorConfigProperties.addAll(properties)

118→        return this

121→    /**
122→     * Set the [EditorConfigOverride] "max_line_length" property based on the EOL Marker which is places at the first
123→     * line of the code sample. If the property has been set before via [withEditorConfigOverride] then that value is
124→     * silently overwritten.
125→     *
126→     * Example of usage:
127→     * ```
128→     *  val code =
129→     *      """
130→     *      // $MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER                   $EOL_CHAR
131→     *      val fooooooooooooooo = "fooooooooooooooooooooo"
132→     *      """.trimIndent()
133→     *  maxLineLengthRuleAssertThat(code)
134→     *      .setMaxLineLength()
135→     *      .hasLintViolation(2, 1, "Exceeded max line length (46)")

137→     */
138→    @Throws(MissingEolMarker::class)
139→    public fun setMaxLineLength(): KtLintAssertThat {
140→        code
141→            .split("\n")
142→            .firstOrNull { it.contains(MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER) && it.endsWith(EOL_CHAR) }
143→            ?.indexOf(EOL_CHAR)
144→            ?.let { index -> editorConfigProperties.add(MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY to (index + 1).toString()) }
145→            ?: throw MissingEolMarker()
146→
147→        return this
148→    }
